calendar.matters:
Hearing Re: Plaintiff'S Motion For Appointment Of Special Master
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion For Appointment Of Special Master:

Based on the parties' joint status report re motion for appointment of special master filed 3/24/21, the Court understands that the parties have agreed that Mr. Chek Tan should be appointed as special master.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Designating Case As Complex And For Appointment Of Special Master And Attached Documents: Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Counsel In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Alan Kwong's Motion For Order Designating Case As Complex And For Appointment Of Special Master.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to designate case complex is denied without prejudice. That application needs to be presented to department 304 per SF Local Rule 3.5C(2).

Plaintiff's motion for appointment of special master is continued to April 1, 2021. The Court determines that there is good cause to appoint a special master/referee as determining who are qualified members of the defendant association, which numbers in the thousands perhaps, will require the examination of a long account. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 639(a)(1).) The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately regarding the selection of a special master. The Court notes that appointing a special master who is fluent in English and Chinese (Mandarin/Cantonese) would likely be most efficacious and cost-effective. If the parties cannot agree, then each party may submit up to 3 nominees by March 24, 2021 per Code of Civil Procedure section 640(b). The parties should also meet and confer regarding the maximum cost of the reference and the precise matters included in the reference. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 639(d)(1)-(6).)

Plaintiff Kwong fails to demonstrate an inability to pay the special master fees so the parties shall split them 50/50. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 639(b)(6).) Each party has leave to file a brief of 5 pages or less, which can include nominees, by March 24, 2021.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
